---
title: "Other LMU Support Services"
format: 
  html:
    css: styles.css
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
listing:
  template: partners.ejs
  contents:
    - name: "Research Funding Unit"
      image_url: "images/lmu_logo.jpg"
      contactName: "Dr. Florian Schreck"
      contactLink: "mailto:florian.schreck@verwaltung.uni-muenchen.de"
      website_url: "https://fdm.ub.lmu.de/services-networks/research-funding/"
      description: |
        The Research Funding Unit is here to help researchers comply to requirements of funding agencies to increase your chances of getting funded.

    - name: "StaBLab: Statistical consulting unit"
      image_url: "images/stablab_logo.jpeg"
      contactName: "StaBLab team"
      contactLink: "mailto:kontakt@stablab.stat.uni-muenchen.de"
      website_url: "https://www.stat.lmu.de/stablab/en/"
      description: |
        The StaBLab team provide free statistical consultations for LMU students and researchers to support them with their thesis and research projects with: 
        <ul>
          <li>Experimental design and sampling planning</li>
          <li>Statistical modelling</li>
          <li>Big Data Analyses & Machine Learning</li>
        </ul>

    - name: "MLCU: Machine Learning Consulting Unit"
      image_url: "images/lmu_logo.jpg"
      contactName: "MLCU"
      contactLink: "https://www.slds.stat.uni-muenchen.de/consulting/webform.html"
      website_url: "https://www.slds.stat.uni-muenchen.de/consulting/"
      description: |
        The MLCU provides machine learning consulting to LMU students and researchers. We offer:
        <ul>
          <li>Help with selection of appropriate methods to answer specific research questions</li>
          <li>Support with implementation and interpretation of machine and deep learning methods</li>
          <li>Support in setup and implementation of reproducible benchmark experiments (including hyperparameter optimization and benchmark evaluation)</li>
        </ul>

    - name: "University Library LMU - Research Data Management Information"
      image_url: "images/ub.JPG"
      contactName: "RDM team"
      contactLink: "mailto:rdm@ub.uni-muenchen.de"
      website_url: "https://www.en.ub.uni-muenchen.de/writing/research_data/index.html"
      description: |
        The RDM Information team at the University Library of LMU Munich provides infrastructure and gives advice on:
        <ul>
          <li>How to start your research data management</li>
          <li>How to write RDM plans (e.g., with the tool RDMO)</li>
          <li>How to make your data FAIR (findable, accessible, interoperable, reusable)</li>
          <li>How to use data repositories, including the infrastructure "Open Data LMU"</li>
        </ul>

    - name: "University Library LMU - Open Access team"
      image_url: "images/ub.JPG"
      contactName: "Open Access team"
      contactLink: "mailto:open-access@ub.uni-muenchen.de"
      website_url: "https://www.en.ub.uni-muenchen.de/writing/open-access-publishing/oa-auxiliary-page/index.html"
      description: |
        The University Library provides:
        <ul>
          <li>Infrastructure to preserve and share articles or books following the Green or Gold Open Access route (e.g., preprint, postprints, articles, monographs): Open Access LMU</li>
          <li>Infrastructure for your own Open Access journal: Open Journals LMU</li>
          <li>Infrastructure to publish your university thesis or monograph in both print and digital form as Open Access publication: Open Publishing LMU</li>
          <li>Funds to cover article or book processing charges: Open Access publishing Fund</li>
        </ul>

    - name: "IT-Gruppe Geisteswissenschaften (ITG) - LMU Center for Digital Humanities"
      image_url: "images/itg.png"
      contactName: "Dr. Stephan Lücke"
      contactLink: "mailto:luecke@lmu.de"
      website_url: "https://www.itg.uni-muenchen.de/index.html"
      description: |
        The ITG provides advice and assistance with:
        <ul>
          <li>Planning and conducting projects with substantial shares of digital methods in the humanities</li>
          <li>Research data management</li>
        </ul>
        The ITG is responsible for accompanying digital projects in the humanities, computer science, statistics and computational linguistics as well as the emerging data sciences.
---

<script>

document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
  document.querySelectorAll('.email-link').forEach(a => {
    const href = (a.getAttribute('href') || '').trim();
    const text = (a.textContent || '').trim();
    const noEmail = !href || href.toLowerCase() === 'mailto:' || (!text || text === '' || text === '\u00A0');

    if (noEmail) {
      const icon = a.querySelector('i.fa-envelope, i.fa-solid.fa-envelope');
      if (icon) icon.remove();
      // optional: also remove empty anchor entirely if you prefer:
      // if (!a.textContent.trim()) a.remove();
    }
  });
});
</script>
